mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is flat or declining, the market should reprice a lower discount rate and easier liquidity conditions, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average during FEDFUNDS downtrends, or if the sign flips and downtrending FEDFUNDS is followed by negative 20-day returns in a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level signals weakening labor demand and a slower earnings outlook, which should pressure S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-threshold episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the conditional mean return is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 percentage point annualized.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous acceleration in RSAFS and PAYEMS indicates broadening nominal demand and labor income, which should support equity sales expectations and push S&P 500 higher over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint RSAFS-PAYEMS signal does not outperform either series alone, or if 60-day forward returns are not positive in the top tercile of the joint growth metric.

mechanism_narrative: An improvement in INDPRO with stable or falling CPIAUCSL indicates easing supply constraints and softer inflation pressure, which should lower real-rate fears and lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change is below 2.5% while INDPRO is rising
failure_condition: Reject if periods with rising INDPRO and sub-2.5% inflation do not show positive average 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term is not materially stronger than either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: A high FEDFUNDS rate above the neutral-looking low-rate regime increases discount rates and tightens financial conditions, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate episodes do not produce negative 5-day SPX returns, or if the conditional hit rate is not below 40% negative returns over triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: If PAYEMS is rising while UNRATE is falling, labor income and household spending power are improving together, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-up and UNRATE-down condition does not yield positive 60-day forward returns more often than not, or if the joint effect is not stronger than either labor series separately.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in GDPC1 after prior expansion signals weakening aggregate demand and future earnings revisions, which should lower S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines versus the prior quarter
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDP contractions are not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns on average, or if the sign of the conditional effect is positive in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock normalization phase, rising INDPRO together with rising HOUST indicates supply-side repair in goods and housing, which should support risk appetite and lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous increases in INDPRO and HOUST do not precede positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the joint signal loses predictive power after accounting for each series alone.
